title_en: Why Minnesota Can’t Do More to Stop ICE
title_zh: 为什么明尼苏达州无法做更多来阻止ICE
rubric_en: Democratic lawmakers have few options that wouldn’t trigger something like civil war.
rubric_zh: 民主党立法者几乎没有任何选择，不做什么都难以避免重演类似内战的局面。

paragraphs.en: America has never seen a moment in modern history like the federal occupation of Minneapolis. Thousands of masked federal officers with uncertain authority are rampaging through the region, assaulting protesters and innocent people, abusing constitutional safeguards, staking out daycares and schools, snatching people off the streets in unmarked vans based on the color of their skin or their accent, and recklessly, relentlessly provoking violent confrontations with civilians—all against the loud, repeatedly expressed wishes of local and state officials.
paragraphs.zh: 美国现代史上从未出现过像联邦势力进驻明尼阿波利斯这样的一幕。成千上万名身份不明、戴着面具、权限存疑的联邦官员正在该地区横行，袭击抗议者和无辜民众，肆意践踏宪法保护，在托儿所和学校外驻守，在街头用未挂牌车辆按肤色或口音抓捕行人，并且鲁莽且不断地挑衅平民，引发暴力对峙——这一切都违背了地方和州政府多次高声、反复表达的意愿。

paragraphs.en: Saturday morning, federal agents—apparently from the Border Patrol—shot and killed a 37-year-old nurse, Alex Pretti, amid a chaotic scuffle in front of a well-known Minneapolis donut store after agents began hassling and shoving him in the street. It was the second time this month that federal agents resorted to deadly force within mere seconds after initiating an encounter with a Minneapolis civilian who never posed a threat to agents.
paragraphs.zh: 周六早晨，联邦特工——似乎来自边境巡逻队——在明尼阿波利斯一家著名甜甜圈店前的混乱扭斗中开枪打死了37岁的护士亚历克斯·普雷蒂。事发经过是，特工先是在街上骚扰、推搡他。这个月这已是联邦特工在与一名并未对他们构成威胁的明尼阿波利斯平民接触后，仅在数秒内再次动用致命武力的第二起事件。

paragraphs.en: All of this unnecessary violence raises the question: Why can’t elected officials do more to stop this? Apart from the courts, doesn’t Minnesota’s government have cards it can play in the battle against the Trump-led occupation, like calling out the National Guard for a showdown with federal agents?
paragraphs.zh: 所有这些非必要的暴力行为都引出了一个问题：为什么民选官员做不了更多来阻止？除法院之外，明尼苏达州政府在对抗特朗普主导的占领时，难道没有什么手段可用，比如召集国民警卫队与联邦特工正面对峙？

paragraphs.en: The answer, in its shortest form, is mostly no, due to the basic foundations of American federalism. Over and over, Minneapolis mayor Jacob Frey and Minnesota governor Tim Walz have asked President Donald Trump to call back his operation. On Saturday, they again told the public that they had pleaded with the federal government to reconsider and withdraw the upwards of 3,000 immigration officers, who together nearly outnumber the region’s 10 largest state and local police departments combined.
paragraphs.zh: 最简短的答案是“多数情况下不行”，原因在于美国联邦主义的基本结构。明尼阿波利斯市长雅各布·弗雷和明尼苏达州州长蒂姆·沃尔兹一再要求唐纳德·特朗普总统叫停这场行动。周六，他们再次向公众表示，自己已恳请联邦政府重新考虑并撤回约3,000名移民执法人员，这一规模几乎超过了该地区前十个州警和地方警力部门的总和。

paragraphs.en: But Walz and Frey haven’t taken overt actions to use state and local governments to actively resist because a key part of federalism in the United States is that a state can’t really resist federal authority or kick out federal law enforcement officials. That principle primarily exists because the federal government is meant to be the “protector” of last resort if local and state officials fail to uphold the rights of ordinary citizens. What’s remarkable, and worrisome, is that Trump today is using that arrangement to punish political opponents based on his own whims, as un-American an action as any president has ever undertaken and one that threatens the very union of the United States.
paragraphs.zh: 但沃尔兹和弗雷并未采取公开行动，动用州和地方政府来积极抵制，因为美国联邦制的一个关键原则是：一个州并不能真正抵抗联邦权威，更不能把联邦执法人员赶走。该原则之所以存在，主要在于联邦政府被设定为“最后的保护者”，一旦地方和州官员未能维护普通公民的权利，就由它来兜底。令人惊讶且令人担忧的是，特朗普今天正是利用这一安排，依据自己的一时兴起去惩罚政治反对者——这是一位总统所做过的任何行为中同样“非美国传统”的做法，也是一种威胁美国联邦共同体本身的行为。

paragraphs.en: Tension in Minnesota between national officials and state and local elected officials is already sky high—and there’s rising animus between the federal forces and local law enforcement as well.
paragraphs.zh: 明尼苏达州国家层面官员与州和地方民选官员之间的紧张关系已经到了前所未有的高度，而且联邦力量与地方执法部门之间也正出现加剧的敌意。

paragraphs.en: Minneapolis police were quick to condemn the shootings of both Pretti and Renee Nicole Good, who was shot and killed by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agent on January 7. They have also been vocal in recent days about how their own officers have been harassed and even assaulted by immigration agents while off-duty. (“Every one of [the off-duty officers stopped by immigration agents] is a person of color,” one police chief complained.) DHS agents tried to bar local police from the shooting scene on Saturday, forcing them to return with a court order, and this time FBI agents again apparently resisted allowing access. “We’re in uncharted territory here,” the head of the state’s investigation bureau said.
paragraphs.zh: 明尼阿波利斯警方很快谴责了针对普雷蒂（Pretti）和雷妮·妮可·古德（Renee Nicole Good）这两起枪击案，后者于1月7日被一名移民与海关执法局（ICE）特工开枪打死。警方最近几天也一直公开指责，在休班期间，他们的警员遭到移民执法人员骚扰，甚至遭到殴打。（“每一位被移民执法人员拦下的休班警员都是有色人种，”一名警察局长在抱怨。）国土安全部（DHS）特工试图在周六阻止地方警察进入枪击现场，迫使他们出示法院命令才得以返回，而这一次联邦调查局（FBI）特工似乎再次抵制放行。州调查局局长说：我们现在处在前所未有的境地。

paragraphs.en: A governor also has the state's National Guard at their disposal, which could hypothetically be deployed to counter ICE's actions. Yesterday, Minnesota governor Tim Walz announced that he activated them following the shooting, amid a tense series of hours that saw the NBA postpone its evening game in Minneapolis for safety reasons. In a statement whose banality and simplicity underplayed just how stunning it was, the governor’s office said the guard was needed because “local law enforcement resources are stretched thin because of the disruption to public safety caused by thousands of federal immigration agents in our neighborhoods.” So far it seems like Walz’s intent is to use the National Guard and local and state police as more of a buffer between federal forces and Minnesota residents, rather than as a supplement to the street resistance efforts.
paragraphs.zh: 州长还可以调动全州国民警卫队，理论上可用来对冲ICE的行动。昨天，明尼苏达州州长提姆·沃尔兹在射击事件后宣布启动国民警卫队，在一段紧张时段中，正是在此背景下，NBA因安全原因取消了在明尼阿波利斯举行的夜场比赛。州长办公室在一份简短平实、几乎掩盖了其分量的声明中说，启用警卫队的原因是：由于数千名联邦移民执法人员在我们社区造成公共安全秩序紊乱，地方执法资源已经捉襟见肘。迄今来看，沃尔兹的意图似乎是把国民警卫队以及州、地方警力更多地当作联邦力量与明尼苏达居民之间的一道缓冲层，而不是作为街头抵抗行动的补充。

paragraphs.en: For now, state and local officials—like those who also suffered from heavy-handed immigration deployments last year, like Los Angeles, Chicago, and elsewhere—are primarily focused on using federal courts for relief. Overnight, a federal judge issued a temporary restraining order to ensure that federal officials didn’t destroy or alter evidence related to Saturday’s shooting. More broadly, Minnesota has a pending lawsuit aimed at blocking the immigration deployments altogether.
paragraphs.zh: 目前，州和地方官员——包括去年也遭遇了同样强硬移民执法部署的洛杉矶、芝加哥以及其他地区的官员——主要集中在通过联邦法院寻求救济。一天夜里，联邦法官签发了临时禁令，确保联邦官员不会销毁或篡改与周六枪击事件有关的证据。更广泛地说，明尼苏达州正在进行一场旨在完全阻止移民执法部署的待审诉讼。

paragraphs.en: This restraint is almost certainly intended to prevent even further escalation, driven by President Trump’s apparent eagerness to declare Minnesota in a state of “insurrection” that would allow him to activate and deploy federal troops against its residents. Trump has already launched unprecedented criminal investigations against Walz and Frey, and others, in an apparent bid to create pretexts that could aid the use of the Insurrection Act.
paragraphs.zh: 这种克制几乎可以肯定是为了防止进一步升级，这种升级在于特朗普总统似乎迫切想要宣布明尼苏达州处于“叛乱”状态，以便他可以动员并部署联邦军队对抗当地居民。特朗普已经对沃尔茨、弗雷伊等人发起了前所未有的刑事调查，显然是在为可能动用《叛乱法案》制造借口。

paragraphs.en: The situation is so unprecedented—and the Trump administration’s political incentives so skewed by social media and the president’s own manias—that it’s impossible to understand or predict how the coming days and weeks might unfold. It’s especially unclear how state or local elected leaders might make the situation better for their constituents and neighbors—and, because of the Trump administration’s lawlessness, there are many paths where the situation worsens.
paragraphs.zh: 局势之所以前所未有——再加上特朗普政府的政治动机被社交媒体和总统本人冲动行为扭曲——使得人们几乎无法理解或预测未来几天、几周将如何发展。尤其不清楚州级或地方民选领导人如何才能让情况对其选民和邻里变得更好，而由于特朗普政府的违法乱纪性，局势恶化的路径有许多。

paragraphs.en: President Trump and his Department of Homeland Security in many ways appear to be attempting to provoke an actual showdown between federal forces and local or state authorities. DHS and White House officials have repeatedly labeled protesters exercising their First Amendment rights in Minnesota “domestic terrorists,” and they were quick to smear the victim of Saturday’s shooting; they shut down an investigation of the ICE officer who killed Renee Nicole Good earlier this month and ordered a federal investigation of Good and her spouse instead, leading to the resignation of the FBI agent assigned to the case; at least six federal prosecutors also resigned, refusing to participate in the attempt to scapegoat a victim.
paragraphs.zh: 从许多方面看，特朗普总统及其国土安全部似乎在试图挑起联邦力量与地方或州当局之间的真正对峙。国土安全部和白宫官员一再将明尼苏达州行使第一修正案权利的抗议者称为“国内恐怖分子”，并且在周六枪击案受害者事件中迅速抹黑受害者；他们停止了对本月早些时候枪杀瑞妮·尼可拉·古德（Renee Nicole Good）的ICE警官的调查，转而命令对古德及其配偶进行联邦调查，导致负责该案的FBI探员辞职；至少有六名联邦检察官也辞职，拒绝参与将受害者当作替罪羊的做法。

paragraphs.en: In a deeply troubling development, ICE agents have started declaring that people who so much as record them are being entered into a national database as “domestic terrorists.” “Have fun with that,” an ICE officer in Maine was captured joking to a civilian filming him. (That the assault on Minneapolis has little to do with violent crime or immigration issues was underscored Saturday when attorney general Pam Bondi sent state officials a letter saying they’d withdraw the federal forces if Minnesota complied with a Trump request to turn over its voter rolls.)
paragraphs.zh: 在一个极为令人担忧的发展中，ICE特工已经开始声明，哪怕只是把人拍下来的行为，也会被录入全国数据库并标记为“国内恐怖分子”。一名在缅因州拍摄他的平民被一名ICE官员拍下画面中听到其开玩笑说：“玩得开心啊。”（这一点在周六再次得到强调：明尼阿波利斯的袭击与暴力犯罪或移民问题几乎无关，明尼苏达州总检察长佩姆·邦迪给州官员写信称，如果明尼苏达州配合特朗普提交选民名册的要求，他方会撤回联邦武装力量。）

paragraphs.en: While Walz and Frey’s seeming passivity in the face of an all-out federal attack on their constituents is provoking anger among activists, it may be a case where caution and restraint is the better part of valor. Citizens are already generating massive resistance to the federal presence in Minnesota, and any additional “official” resistance by local or state officials may tip the scales for Trump and bring federal troops into the picture, an escalation that would be surely worse for residents, the state, and the nation at large. Trump has already put some 1,500 federal troops in Alaska on standby to deploy to Minnesota, and all indications are that he’s eager for an excuse to do so.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管沃尔兹和弗雷伊在全国性攻势面前对其选民所采取的“袖手旁观”行为正在激起活动人士的愤怒，但这可能正是谨慎和克制更为明智的地方。公民已经对联邦力量在明尼苏达的存在展开了大规模抵抗，而地方或州官员再增添任何“官方”层面的抵抗，都可能使局势在特朗普一方倾斜，并把联邦军队带入其中——这将无疑更加伤害居民、州政府以及整个国家。特朗普已将大约1,500名联邦军队在阿拉斯加待命，准备部署到明尼苏达，且一切迹象表明他急于寻找这样做的借口。

paragraphs.en: Any such declaration of “insurrection” or deployment of federal troops to further subdue local and state authorities would bring the country the closest it’s been to civil war since, well, the Civil War.
paragraphs.zh: 任何此类“叛乱”宣告，或向进一步压制地方和州当局而部署联邦军队的举动，都将使美国接近自那场内战以来最危险、最接近内战的状态。

paragraphs.en: Under normal circumstances, the use of federal military forces in domestic situations is highly proscribed and limited by two sets of law, the Posse Comitatus Act of 1878—which prohibits the use of the military in civilian law enforcement—and the Insurrection Act, a series of statutes that date to 1792 and outline when a president can call out troops to suppress rebellions and domestic violence.
paragraphs.zh: 在正常情况下，联邦军队在国内场景中的使用受到严格限制，并受两套法律约束：1878年的《平定暴乱法案》（Posse Comitatus Act），该法禁止军队参与民事执法；以及一系列可追溯到1792年的《镇压叛乱法》相关法条，规定了总统在何种情况下可以动用军队来镇压叛乱和国内暴力。

paragraphs.en: Considering how critical a principle it guards, the Insurrection Act is badly antiquated, as recent reform efforts have shown. As national security legal scholar Scott Anderson wrote in 2024, “Few principles of American government are more foundational than the idea that the US military should not be used against Americans, except in the most dire of circumstances. But even fewer foundational principles are so loosely grounded in the law … The fact that past presidents have done so relatively rarely is more a product of long-standing political norms than hard legal limits.”
paragraphs.zh: 鉴于它所捍卫的是一项极其关键的原则，叛乱法如今已显得极端过时，最近的改革努力已经证明了这一点。正如国家安全法学者斯科特·安德森（Scott Anderson）在2024年写道：“在美国政府的原则中，没有哪条比‘美国军队不得对美国人使用，除非在极端危险的情况下’更具基础性。但也几乎没有哪条更基础的原则在法律上有如此松散的依据……过去总统之所以很少这样做，更像是长期政治惯例的结果，而不是严格法律边界所决定的。”

paragraphs.en: While state National Guard units and federal troops are routinely used to help respond to natural disasters at the request of and in coordination with local and state officials, since Reconstruction presidents have only reluctantly ever sent in federal agents or troops against the wishes of state governments. Even then, it’s in highly limited circumstances, such as defending civil rights and freedoms in moments when state officials refused to do so. (National Guard units usually report to state governors, although they can be federalized by presidential order to report to the commander-in-chief instead—meaning that Trump could always circumvent Walz activating them by putting them under direct Pentagon control anyway.)
paragraphs.zh: 虽然州国民警卫队和联邦军队通常会在地方和州官员的请求与协调下，常规性地用于应对自然灾害，但自重建时期以来，总统仅在州政府反对时勉强才会派遣联邦特工或军队。这种做法也只是发生在极为有限的情形下，例如在州官员拒绝履责时，维护公民权利和自由。 （州国民警卫队通常向州长汇报，尽管总统可通过总统命令将其“联邦化”，令其改向总司令部队汇报——这意味着特朗普始终可以通过将其置于五角大楼直接指挥之下来绕过沃尔兹对其的激活。）

paragraphs.en: In fact, while National Guard troops were used repeatedly in the 20th century by governors and presidents to break labor strikes—often brutally—it wasn’t until the Civil Rights movement and the 1960s that federal troops began to play a more routine role in responding to civil disturbances.
paragraphs.zh: 事实上，虽然20世纪国民警卫队部队多次被州长和总统用于镇压罢工——通常手段相当残酷——但直到民权运动和20世纪60年代，联邦军队才开始在应对骚乱中扮演更常态化的角色。

paragraphs.en: In 1957, when Arkansas governor Orval Faubus used the state’s National Guard to block the integration of Little Rock Central High School, President Dwight Eisenhower responded by federalizing all 10,000 Arkansas National Guardsmen and deploying troops from the 101st Airborne Division to force the school’s integration and protect the nine Black students attempting to enroll there. The mission was a key learning opportunity for the military, and when the Army manual on “Civil Disturbances” was republished in 1958, it had doubled in size and now contained a new section on riot control.
paragraphs.zh: 1957年，当阿肯色州州长奥瓦尔·福布斯（Orval Faubus）动用该州国民警卫队阻挠小石城中学（Little Rock Central High School）实现种族融合时，总统德怀特·艾森豪威尔（Dwight Eisenhower）遂作出回应：将阿肯色州全部一万名国民警卫队员“联邦化”，并派遣第101空降师兵力进驻，强制执行学校的融合并保护九名试图入学的黑人学生。该行动对军队而言是一次关键的学习契机，1958年《军队纪律/民事骚乱手册》再版时，内容规模翻倍，并新增了一个关于防暴控制的新章节。

paragraphs.en: In every year of his short presidency, President John F. Kennedy deployed federal marshals or troops against the wishes of state officials as the civil rights movement gained momentum. In 1961, some 400 federal marshals were sent to Alabama to protect the Freedom Riders, and when James Meredith attempted to enroll as the first Black student at the University of Mississippi in 1962, Kennedy sent federal marshals who then faced incredibly violent white protesters.
paragraphs.zh: 在约翰·F·肯尼迪短暂总统任期的每一年，随着民权运动势头增强，他都在州官员反对的情况下出动联邦法警或军队。1961年，大约400名联邦法警被派往阿拉巴马州，以保护“自由乘车者”；1962年，当詹姆斯·默里迪思试图作为第一位黑人成为密西西比大学学生入学时，肯尼迪派出了联邦法警，而他们随后遭遇了异常暴力的白人抗议者。

paragraphs.en: With the marshals under attack, Kennedy deployed first the Mississippi National Guard and then thousands of federal troops as well. (That military operation, codenamed RAPID ROAD, was actually the first and only time during the Cold War that the military activated and used plans it had developed to quell civil disturbances in the wake of a nuclear attack.)
paragraphs.zh: 在法警遭到攻击后，肯尼迪先是调动了密西西比国民警卫队，随后又出动了成千上万名联邦部队。 （这场代号为“迅捷公路行动”（RAPID ROAD）的军事行动，实际上是在冷战期间，军方首次也是唯一一次激活并使用其制定的、用于在核打击后遏制内乱的应急计划。）

paragraphs.en: Then, in 1963, Kennedy again relied on the National Guard to help with the integration of the University of Alabama, and his successor, Lyndon Johnson, used marshals and the National Guard to protect civil rights marchers in Selma after Alabama state troopers infamously attacked them at the Edmund Pettus Bridge in an incident that came to be known as “Bloody Sunday.”
paragraphs.zh: 随后在1963年，肯尼迪再次依靠国民警卫队来推进阿拉巴马大学的种族融合，他的继任者林登·约翰逊则在阿拉巴马州国土巡逻队在埃德蒙·佩托斯桥上臭名昭著地攻击示威者、也就是后来被称为“血腥星期天”的事件后，动用了法警和国民警卫队来保护塞尔玛的民权游行者。

paragraphs.en: Presidents began using military troops, including the National Guard, more routinely in America’s cities in the 1960s. During summer riots following police brutality in Detroit in 1967, President Johnson ordered elements of the 82nd and 101st Airborne Divisions into the city, and Michigan governor George Romney called up the Michigan National Guard; more than 40 people were killed, more than half by Detroit police. National Guard troops killed 11, including a 4-year-old girl, Tanya Blanding, who died when a Michigan guardsman opened fire with a tank-mounted.50-caliber machine gun on her apartment after wrongly believing a sniper was inside.
paragraphs.zh: 20世纪60年代，美国总统开始更常规地在城市中动用军队，包括国民警卫队。1967年夏天底特律警察暴力事件后引发骚乱时，约翰逊总统命令第82空降师和第101空降师的一部分部队进城，密歇根州州长乔治·罗姆尼也调用了密歇根国民警卫队；超过40人丧生，其中一半以上死于底特律警察之手。国民警卫队又杀害了11人，其中包括4岁的女孩塔尼娅·布兰丁（Tanya Blanding），她在一名密歇根警卫队员误以为其公寓内有狙击手后，因那名士兵开火使用装在坦克上的.50口径机枪而丧生。

paragraphs.en: While troops were used again amid the 1968 riots that followed the assassination of Martin Luther King, Jr., the downside and risk of such deployments was vividly captured two years later at Kent State University when National Guard troops opened fire on students protesting the Vietnam War, killing four and wounding nine.
paragraphs.zh: 1968年在马丁·路德·金恩牧师遇刺后的骚乱中再次动用了军队，而这种部署的负面后果和风险，在两年后肯特州立大学再次显现：国民警卫队向反对越南战争的学生开火，打死了四人、打伤了九人。

paragraphs.en: Over the years since, there has been incredibly limited domestic use of federal troops—the Los Angeles riots of 1992 being one exception—and presidents and attorneys general until the Trump administration usually go out of their way to coordinate surges of federal law enforcement to cities or states.
paragraphs.zh: 此后数年里，美国国内动用联邦军队的情况一直非常有限——1992年洛杉矶骚乱是一个例外——并且历任总统和司法部长在特朗普政府之前，通常都会尽量协调联邦执法力量向城市或州的增援。

paragraphs.en: Even during the peak of the marshals and troop deployments to the South amid the Civil Rights movement, presidents only acted after state officials either refused to quell violence targeting Americans practicing their constitutional rights or, in the case of the Alabama state troopers, were the cause of the violence against peaceful citizens themselves. Often, a president acted only after there was defiance on the ground of a lawful court order—ensuring that there was a second branch of government acting as a check-and-balance and trigger for such federal action.
paragraphs.zh: 即使在民权运动期间，联邦法警和军队向南方州大量部署的高峰期，总统也只在州官员要么拒绝平息针对行使宪法权利的美国人所面临的暴力，要么在阿拉巴马州的州警自身就是针对和平公民施暴的根源时才会采取行动。通常，总统是在有法庭合法命令遭到实地公开违抗后才出手，以确保已有另一权力分支作为制衡并触发这类联邦行动。

paragraphs.en: While Trump has said that the immigration enforcement effort in Minneapolis—as with previous efforts in Los Angeles, Washington, DC, Chicago, Charlotte, Portland and, most recently, Maine—is meant to enforce “law and order,” there’s no apparent rhyme, reason, or necessity to the deployments beyond political terror.
paragraphs.zh: 虽然特朗普声称，在明尼阿波利斯的移民执法行动——与他此前在洛杉矶、华盛顿特区、芝加哥、夏洛特、波特兰以及最近的缅因州的行动一样——是为了“维护法律和秩序”，但除政治恐吓之外，这些部署似乎找不到任何连贯的理由、依据或必要性。

paragraphs.en: Trump today is attempting something unprecedented that stands in contravention of all historical tradition in the United States: the brutal application of federal forces against a state and region with no apparent reason beyond its being led by members of the political opposition.
paragraphs.zh: 特朗普今天正在进行一项前所未有的举动，这种做法背离了美国所有历史传统：对一个州和一个地区动用联邦武力进行高压打压，表面上没有任何正当理由，唯一原因竟是该州由政治反对派成员执政。

paragraphs.en: In deploying immigration officers and border security agents from the DHS, rather than deputy US marshals from the Department of Justice—as presidents in the past have done—Trump is also changing the nature and tenor of his federal force. Marshals, whose work and training involves constitutional rights and protections, have always been used to protect civil rights and valid court orders and come with strong federal policing powers and authorities. The agents from Customs and Border Protection (CBP) and ICE are different. They are not trained to normal federal law enforcement standards of dealing with the public and are meant to operate with severely limited authority to enforce immigration matters, not general federal laws. CBP agents in particular are less a regular law enforcement agency, grounded in due process, and more a paramilitary force meant to operate on the border regions. They were never intended to have regular contact with US citizens and civilians.
paragraphs.zh: 在部署移民官员和国土安全部（DHS）的边境安全人员，而不是像历任总统那样派遣司法部的美国法警时，特朗普也在改变联邦力量的性质和气质。法警的工作与训练涉及宪法权利与保障，历来被用于保护公民权利和有效法院命令，并具有强大的联邦执法权力和权限。美国移民与海关执法局（CBP）和移民与海关执法局（ICE）的特工则不同。它们不按一般联邦执法部门对公众接触和处置的标准进行训练，且本应以有限授权为主，只负责执行移民事务，而非一般联邦法律。特别是CBP特工，他们更像边境地区的准军事力量，而不是受正当程序约束的常规执法机构；他们从未被设想为与美国公民和普通民众保持常态接触。

paragraphs.en: Trump has also attempted to use troops in similar crackdowns over the last year and been stymied by federal courts, who, among other instances, preliminarily blocked his federalization of the California National Guard.
paragraphs.zh: 过去一年里，特朗普也曾尝试在类似的镇压行动中使用军队，但在联邦法院面前受阻，其中包括法院在若干案件中先行裁定，暂时阻止了他将加利福尼亚国民警卫队联邦化的做法。

paragraphs.en: Invoking the Insurrection Act might give Trump cover to circumvent such restrictions.
paragraphs.zh: 援引《叛乱法》可能为特朗普提供掩护，以绕开此类限制。

paragraphs.en: Today, the Insurrection Act has three potential triggers: one in cooperation with state officials and two against the wishes of state officials. Trump, the DHS, and the Justice Department appear to be attempting to artificially manufacture some combination of actions that would allow them to declare Minnesota as representing “unlawful obstructions, combinations, or assemblages, or rebellion” or beset by “insurrection, domestic violence, unlawful combination, or conspiracy” that “so hinders the execution of the laws” that federal military reinforcements are necessary.
paragraphs.zh: 如今，反叛法案有三种可能触发情形：一种是与州官员合作，另外两种是违背州官员意愿。特朗普、国土安全部（DHS）和司法部似乎正在试图人为拼凑出某种行动组合，以便据此宣布明尼苏达州正处于“违法阻挠、串联或聚集，或叛乱”，或遭受“叛乱、国内暴力、非法结盟或阴谋”，从而“严重妨碍法律执行”，以此为依据认为有必要派遣联邦军力增援。

paragraphs.en: If Walz, Frey, and other state officials seem reluctant to do anything other than use words to oppose Trump, it’s surely because they’re wary of what unrest and conflict would come from giving Trump any excuse to declare an “insurrection”—and the precedent any court battle over the vague words of the Insurrection Act might end up creating.
paragraphs.zh: 如果沃尔兹、弗雷和其他州官员似乎只愿意用言辞反对特朗普，理由无疑是他们担忧一旦给特朗普任何借口来宣布“叛乱”，由此引发的骚乱与冲突将会非常严重；更不用说围绕反叛法案模糊措辞引发的一场诉讼，最终可能树立的先例。

paragraphs.en: As one reform effort recently concluded, “Under the Insurrection Act, a president’s authority to deploy US troops turns on terms—including unlawful ‘combinations,’ ‘obstructions,’ and ‘assemblages’—that lack settled contemporary meaning.”
paragraphs.zh: 正如一次改革进程最近的结论所示，“根据反叛法案，总统动员美军的权限取决于一些用词——包括‘非法的‘结盟’、‘阻挠’和‘聚集’——这些词在当代并没有统一明确的法律含义。”

paragraphs.en: In the meantime, even as ICE and CBP’s trail of violence continues, the residents of Minneapolis might find that they’re on their own to face the full weight of Trump’s masked secret police. But the spreading and growing resistance to the Trump administration shows that the protesters of Minnesota are hardly alone nationally. Their courage in the face of the federal assault is contagious. Hours after the shooting Saturday in Minneapolis, at a vigil and protest rally in Boston, a crowd assembled in chilly temperatures ahead of a historic weekend storm and chanted, “We’re not cold, we’re not afraid, Minny taught us to be brave!”
paragraphs.zh: 与此同时，尽管 ICE 与边境与海关执法局（CBP）的暴力行为仍在持续，明尼阿波利斯居民可能会发现自己不得不独自面对特朗普披着“秘密警察”面具的全面压力。与此同时，对特朗普政府持续扩大的抵抗正在蔓延并增长，这表明明尼苏达的抗议者在全国范围内并不孤立。他们在联邦武装压力下表现出的勇气具有很强的传染性。周六在明尼阿波利斯发生枪击后几个小时，波士顿的一场守夜与抗议集会上，人群在一场历史性周末风暴来临前聚集于寒冷中高呼：“我们不怕冷，我们不害怕，明尼让我们更勇敢！”

paragraphs.en: Let us know what you think about this article. Submit a letter to the editor at [email protected].
paragraphs.zh: 请告诉我们您对这篇文章的看法。请将致编辑信发送至 [email protected]。
